analyst_observations: Eval 1 (large monolith) is the discriminating test: with_skill 6/6 vs without_skill 2/6. The skill's key value is .claude/rules/ routing + severity tiers + primacy/recency — things the baseline doesn't know about.
Eval 2 (add rule) is non-discriminating: both score 4/4. Simple rule additions don't benefit much from the skill since Claude already knows to put Go rules in backend.md.

Eval 3 (negative rules) is also non-discriminating: both score 5/5. Claude naturally converts negative rules well without a skill — the skill adds structural guidance (tiers, primacy/recency) but both versions achieve this.
Token/time tradeoff: with_skill uses ~23% more tokens and ~59% more time. The skill overhead is acceptable given the quality improvement on complex tasks (eval 1).
The skill's unique value proposition is clear for large CLAUDE.md optimization (routing pattern + .claude/rules/) — this is knowledge Claude doesn't have by default.
